articles.full_text:
Home |News |Mancherials Chennur Sizzles At 44 3 Degrees C
Heatwave grips erstwhile Adilabad, farm labourer dies of suspected sunstroke
Severe heatwave conditions prevailed across erstwhile Adilabad district, with temperatures touching 44.3°C in several areas. A farm labourer died of suspected sunstroke in Bheempur mandal, as extreme heat forced residents indoors and disrupted daily activities.
Adilabad: Parts of erstwhile Adilabad district saw scorching heat wave conditions on Friday. The sweltering heat wave claimed the life of a farm laborer.
As per weather report available on the website of Telangana Planning Development Society, Kommera village in Chennur mandal of Mancherial district registered the maximum temperature by 44.3 degrees Celsius as against the average maximum temperature of 41.8 degrees C. Dasturabad in Nirmal district also recorded the same maximum temperature, while Asifabad mandal had the maximum temperature of 44.2 degrees C.
Similarly, Adilabad Urban mandal witnessed the maximum temperature of 43.3 degrees Celsius. People were forced indoors between 11 am and 4 pm. They stayed hydrated by consuming fresh fruit juices, ice apples and watermelons to beat the heat. Roads wore a deserted look. Students were seen carrying umbrellas to avoid sultry weather. Coal mines and opencast projects of SCCL saw a dip in coal production.
Meanwhile, Tekam Gajanan (43), a farm laborer, died of sunstroke at Arli (T) village in Bheempur mandal. He was engaged in making arrangements for a wedding at the time of the incident. He was immediately shifted to RIMS-Adilabad, but doctors declared that he was brought dead. He is survived by a wife and two sons.

articles.title: Telangana heatwave peaks at 45°C, storms to bring relief from April 28 - Hyderabad Mail
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
HYDERABAD: A severe heatwave is expected to intensify across north Telangana over the next three days, with temperatures likely to touch 45°C in isolated areas, according to weather updates.
Districts including Adilabad, Kumuram Bheem Asifabad, Nirmal, Mancherial and Nizamabad are forecast to face extreme heat conditions. Day temperatures in these regions may rise sharply, posing health risks to residents.
In Hyderabad, temperatures are expected to reach up to 43°C at a few locations during the same period.
Thunderstorms likely after peak heat phase
Weather conditions are expected to shift from April 28, bringing some respite. Thunderstorm activity is likely to begin in south and west Telangana before gradually spreading to other regions, including Hyderabad.
Gradual cooling expected across state
The change in weather pattern is likely to bring down temperatures after the peak heat phase. However, officials advise caution during the ongoing heatwave period.
Residents have been urged to take precautions, avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight, and stay hydrated.
